title: Photography
text:
As a wizard and scholar of Middle-earth, I have been studying the magic of the natural world for centuries. Through my self-portraits, I aim to capture the essence of my own being and reflect on my own journey through time. Each photograph is a reflection of my own experiences and emotions. Through my photography, I hope to offer a glimpse into my life as a scholar and adventurer, and inspire others to reflect on their own journeys through the world.

title: Research
text:
Zewei Kong, Kong. Z, P. A. Parker, & S. H. Holan (in preparation)
This paper introduces a Bayesian area-level small-area estimation (SAE) framework that jointly models binomial and Gaussian survey data using shared spatial random effects. To maintain computational efficiency for high-dimensional spatial data, we integrate Polya-Gamma data augmentation with spatial basis functions for scalable Gibbs sampling. Applied to the American Community Survey (ACS), our multi-type model borrows strength across variables to produce highly precise joint estimates of median income and poverty rates.



Zewei Kong, Kong. Z, J. Bradley, P. A. Parker, & S. H. Holan (in preparation)
Focused on unit-level dependent responses, we proposed a new model employing the Gibbs sampler to enhance performance and computational efficiency. The approach implements pseudo-likelihood techniques within a Bayesian framework, demonstrating improved performance on large-scale datasets such as the ACS Public Use Microdata Sample for Illinois, analyzing over 98,000 unit records.

title: Applications
text:
TraffIQ
TraffIQ is an interactive RShiny map application designed for real-time traffic hotspot visualization and route risk assessment. Under the hood, it utilizes spatial point process models to analyze historical accident data and estimate regional risk intensity. Innovatively, TraffIQ integrates an “AI brain” to translate these complex spatial statistical outputs into intuitive, natural-language safety insights, achieving a seamless fusion of advanced statistics and artificial intelligence through a user-friendly UI/UX design.
 Explore TraffIQ →

title: Welcome to My Website
text:
Github
  
  
    
     LinkedIn
  
  
    
     Email
  




Welcome to My Website
I am Zewei Kong, a Ph.D. candidate at the University of Missouri specializing in Bayesian methodology. My current research involves developing novel Bayesian hierarchical frameworks to jointly model multi-type survey data for small area estimation. Alongside a strong theoretical foundation, I am highly proficient in R and Python for full-stack data processing and visualization.
I am also interested in the intersection of traditional statistics, Machine Learning, and AI. A recent highlight is TraffIQ, an RShiny web app I developed that translates complex spatial statistical outputs into intuitive safety insights via an AI backend and a user-friendly UI/UX design built from scratch.
Interested in Bayesian modeling, Machine Learning, or data analytics? Please feel free to contact me.
